channel.title: Home on Igor Kopka
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Igor Kopka

item.title: Real-Time Sign Language Detector Using OpenCV & MediaPipe
How I passed Security+ in 2 weeks and how you can too
item.description: Disclaimer # Before sitting this exam I already had a decent grasp of some of the contents the exam includes from college and university. However, I believe that if you put the time in you could achieve a similar result with no prior knowledge. Exam Basics # Start by watching the entire Professor Messer playlist, as it covers every single topic on the exam objectives. You should also keep track of the objectives while watching the videos.

item.title: Y86 Disassembler
item.description: One of the first modules we took during my first year was a Computer Organisation and Architecture module where we explored how computers work on the low-level. To put everything we learnt into practice, we built a Y86 Disassembler in C. The mini-project was split into three objectives: Decode opcodes to mnemonics, Parse operands and registers from instructions, Disassemble full Y86 Programs from raw bytecode. By the time I finished all three tasks I gained deeper appreciation for how things run on the machine level, you can check the project out here :)
